name: Savory Kasha Stuffing for Poultry
recipeInstructions:
Kasha is frequently called buckwheat groats. It is made from buckwheat grain and then roasted, which gives it a delicious nut-like flavor. Aside from being a tasty stuffing for poultry, this recipe makes an excellent side dish in place of rice, potatoes, or noodles. So if you have any stuffing left over, be sure to refrigerate it and save for a future meal.
1. Mix the groats with beaten egg.
2. Add the mixture to a frying pan over high heat.
3. Stir constantly until grains separate.
4. Add the briskly boiling water, cover the pan, reduce heat, and simmer for 30 minutes.
5. Meanwhile, fry bacon in another large frying pan.
6. When the bacon is lightly browned, push it to one side and add butter.
7. Let the butter sizzle, then add onion, garlic, green pepper, and mushrooms.
8. Stir well while they are cooking so that they do not become too brown.
9. Add oregano, sage, salt, and pepper.
10. Reduce heat and add the cooked groats.
11. Mix well, adjust seasoning, and remove from heat.
This is enough for a small roast chicken and more than enough for 2 Cornish game hens. Increase ingredients for goose.
